descript.abstract: This data includes offices where Forest Service employees work or where IT equipment is housed. There is no Personally Identifiable Information (PII) data in this dataset, nor telework locations. It includes owned, leased and shared offices. Shared offices are buildings owned or leased by another entity (i.e. a university, other federal agency, etc.) but one or more Forest Service employee(s) work at the building or IT equipment is housed at the building.
descript.purpose: Depicts the spatial locations for Office locations from the Forest Service CIO Asset Management Office. It includes owned, leased and shared offices.

idinfo.useconst: The USDA Forest Service makes no warranty, expressed or implied, including the warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose, nor assumes any legal liability or responsibility for the accuracy, reliability, completeness or utility of these geospatial data, or for the improper or incorrect use of these geospatial data. These geospatial data and related maps or graphics are not legal documents and are not intended to be used as such. The data and maps may not be used to determine title, ownership, legal descriptions or boundaries, legal jurisdiction, or restrictions that may be in place on either public or private land. Natural hazards may or may not be depicted on the data and maps, and users should exercise due caution. The data are dynamic and may change over time. The user is responsible to verify the limitations of the geospatial data and to use the data accordingly.

dataqual.logic: Validity of latitude and longitude values are verified on load into the EDW. Data are reviewed and validated by the CIO Asset Management Office
dataqual.complete: Data is complete as of publication.

procstep.procdesc: 1.) Data is collected, maintained and stewarded by the CIO Asset Management Office. 2.) EDW data loading tools extract data the office location data from the CIO Asset Mgt. database. 3.) Latitude and longitude values are validated and then converted to spatial point data. 4.) Spatial point data and associated attributed data describing the office location are inserted into the Office Location Feature class in the Enterprise Data Warehouse. 5.) Changes to the Office Location data are checked daily by EDW data loading tools.

enttyp.enttypd: Feature class: A collection of geographic features with the same geometry type (such as point, line, or polygon), the same attributes, and the same spatial reference.

attr.attrdef: Is physical office only open seasonally
Longitude measurement of location derived from Google Maps

attrdomv.udom: mailing zip code as a string

attr.attrdef: The Administrative Forest Service Region, Station or Area of the location
Site has equipment only, no people
Is mailing address different from physical address?

edom.edomvd: Physical and Mailing Address are different
Physical and Mailing address are the same

attr.attrdef: Street Address (field is normalized to standardize)

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.
5 to 9 digit zip code as string

attr.attrdef: Latitude measurement of location derived from Google Maps

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

distinfo.distliab: The U.S. Forest Service makes no warranty, express or implied, nor assumes any liability or responsibility for the accuracy, reliability, completeness, or utility of these geospatial data or for the improper or incorrect use of those data. The data are dynamic and may change over time. The user is responsible for verifying the limitations of the geospatial data and for using the data accordingly.
